---
title: "AI-Assisted Development"
date-modified: 2026-05-19
---

Software development is changing faster than any framework or language ever
has. AI coding assistants have moved from autocomplete curiosities to agents
capable of planning, writing, and reviewing entire features autonomously.
This module guides you how to work *with* that shift rather than around it,
covering the mental models, tools, and workflows that let developers stay in
control while letting AI handle the repetitive, mechanical, and
context-switching parts of the job.

<!--
Spec-Driven Development, the third stage of generative AI assisted development.

1. [vibe-coding](vibe-coding.qmd)
2. [agentic dev]()
3. [sdd](sdd.qmd)
    - [Get Started with Spec Driven Development](get-started-sdd.qmd)

## Tools

### General

- [skills](skills.qmd)
- ACP Agent Client Protocol
- [MCP](mcp.qmd)

### Specific

- [GitHub Copilot](copilot.qmd)
- Claude
- Codex
- Opencode

## Workflow

= self-contained repo or monorepo ?

### Building local AI Agent

- Ollama
- Opencode

## Ressources

- Monorepo permet de bénéficier d’un meilleur contexte pour les agents IA
= solution: git submodule => monorepo

# TODO

- https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/github-copilot/improving-token-efficiency-in-github-agentic-workflows/
- https://github.com/resources/articles
- https://blog.k10s.dev/im-going-back-to-writing-code-by-hand/
-->
